metadata.seed: I just feel Helpless and hopeless, really. Like I said, just finding a job. I really don't know what I want to do anymore. After I graduated, I thought I was gonna get into business, but I just really don't know if that's for me. My parents always kind of gave me everything too. So the reason why I have my own apartment is because they bought it for me. But it was under the conditions of having to be in the same town as them. And so it's like I'm at a loss and I could continue just loving off my parents money, but then that kind of feels. I feel like I don't have a purpose. Like my purpose really just going to be to live off my parents money and kind of do whatever they want me to do and just to keep their money and their resources. So I'm just kind of at a loss right now in my life.

Like my purpose really just going to be to live off my parents money and kind of do whatever they want me to do and just to keep their money and their resources. So I'm just kind of at a loss right now in my life.

triggers: living in the same town as parents
automatic_thoughts: I don't know what I want to do anymore
business may not be for me
I'm just living off my parents' money

entailment: Loss of hope for the future (finding)

llm_response.response:
Okay, it sounds like you're experiencing a lot of difficult emotions right now – helplessness and hopelessness are really prominent. It's understandable to feel that way when you're facing big decisions about your future, especially when you're questioning a path you thought you wanted to take, like business. It also sounds incredibly frustrating to feel like your living situation, while seemingly providing comfort, is also tied to expectations and a sense of obligation from your parents.

It's tough to grapple with feeling like you're living off your parents and that it might define your purpose. That feeling of being at a loss, of not having a clear direction, is really significant. It seems like you're caught between wanting independence and feeling indebted to your parents, and that's creating a lot of internal conflict.

Let's explore this a bit more. Can you tell me, when you say you "don't know what you want to do anymore," what thoughts are going through your mind at that moment?

validate_and_reflect.response: It sounds like you're experiencing a lot of difficult emotions right now, feeling helpless and hopeless as you navigate this transition and try to figure out your path. It takes courage to acknowledge these feelings and to talk about the complicated dynamic with your parents.

It's like there's this comparison in my mind, and I can't help but feel like I’m falling short. It’s frustrating because I don’t want to feel this way, but then those thoughts just spiral.

patient.query: Um, yeah, I think it really stems from how I grew up. My family always celebrated achievements, big and small, and there was this sense that you had to prove yourself to earn love and respect. I remember moments when my parents praised me for good grades or accomplishments, but it felt like they didn’t really notice me otherwise. So I guess I learned to associate doing well with being valued. It’s just hard to shake off that mindset, even when I know it’s not healthy.
